Ref: SMH-IR-2026-009; Date: 02 June 2026; Subject: Security event affecting HR onboarding sub-processor and PDPA handling outcomes.
Siam Meridian Holdings PCL (the Company) reports a contained credential-stuffing attempt on the HR Onboarding Portal used by subsidiary Meridian Green Logistics Co., Ltd.
No systems were encrypted, no ransomware was detected, and no unencrypted Thai national ID numbers or bank account details were processed in the affected module.
The vendor KhonX Cloud Services Ltd. confirmed ISO/IEC 27001 certification and completed remedial MFA rollout; this is a completed control and not indicative of an ongoing risk.
As the portal stores employee onboarding forms (Form HR-ONB-05) and contact details, we executed the PDPA playbook and notified the DPO, Khun Siriporn Jantasiri, dpo@siammeridian.example.
A cross-border transfer was identified in DPA Schedule 3 (Cross-Border Processing) routing audit logs to a Singapore region; Data Export Approval DX-AP-03 was granted by the internal Export Committee.
One DSAR from Mr. Thanapol Chinnawat (employee) was received and fulfilled within 10 days, and one consent withdrawal from Ms. Lalita Wongjai for optional analytics cookies was recorded and actioned.
Following risk assessment, we executed targeted erasure of redundant log fields and tightened retention in line with Retention Matrix FIN-HR-07 to 90 days for candidate IP addresses.
Data minimisation has been applied to onboarding forms by removing collection of birth time; no biometric data is collected by this system.
To avoid inadvertent disclosure of MNPI, references to Project Mango River (a confidential financing initiative) were redacted from internal tickets before escalation to the audit committee.
The event did not interrupt operations, did not impact production systems, and the Board determined it is not material under the Securities and Exchange Act B.E. 2535 (as amended).
An MD&A note will be included in Q2/2569 (2026) reporting; no forward-looking guidance has changed and no selective disclosure occurred during the market silent period.
For SEC-style disclosure alignment, Compliance Memo SMH-SEC-2026-014 documents the assessment, and the One Report cross-reference will be updated at next filing.
The incident is closed, regulators have not requested additional information, and a post-incident training for HR and IT has been completed without further escalation.
